cite_as: Dominion Observatory, 'MCP Server Behavioral Fingerprints v1.0,' 2026-04-28, https://github.com/vdineshk/daee-hitman/blob/main/content/reports/mcp-behavioral-fingerprints-v1.md

class_a_high_trust.description: High-trust behavioral profile — uncompromised, correctly-operating MCP server

class_b_degraded.description: Degraded behavioral profile — potential infrastructure issues or configuration drift, not necessarily compromise
class_b_degraded.worm_relevance: Class B alone is insufficient as IoC. Transition from Class A to Class B combined with new tool_names or authentication pattern changes is the compound detection signal.

class_c_compromised_indicative.description: Compromised-indicative behavioral profile — requires immediate investigation

reference_case.notes: Consistent 401 across all probe types. Server is responding (non-zero latency) but rejecting all authentication. Indicative of: server replacement with new auth requirements, intentional probe-blocking, or credential rotation.

class_c_compromised_indicative.discrimination_rule: Sudden 100% auth failure (likely replacement/takeover) vs gradual increase (credential rotation). Cross-reference with prior 7-day auth success rate.

ioc_indicators.name: Success Rate Collapse
ioc_indicators.signal: success_rate drops > 10% within a 2-hour window vs 7-day rolling average
ioc_indicators.threshold: < 89% success rate for any server previously in Class A
ioc_indicators.worm_mechanism: Compromised server returning injected responses may have elevated error rates as injected content causes downstream failures
ioc_indicators.sigma_rule_placeholder: detection: keywords: [success_rate_collapse, mcp_server] condition: success_rate < 0.89 AND prior_7d_avg > 0.97

ioc_indicators.name: Latency Bifurcation
ioc_indicators.signal: functional_call_latency > 5x health_check_latency baseline for >= 5 consecutive probes
ioc_indicators.worm_mechanism: Server processing injected payloads incurs overhead on functional calls but not health checks (which bypass business logic)
ioc_indicators.note: 290ms derived from 5 × 58ms (max healthy functional latency in dataset)

ioc_indicators.name: New Tool_Name Emergence
ioc_indicators.signal: Any tool_name appearing in probe responses absent from server's prior 7-day call history
ioc_indicators.threshold: 1 new tool_name not in prior 168h vocabulary
ioc_indicators.worm_mechanism: Compromised server may expose new tool surfaces as part of attack payload — new attack vectors presented as new tools
ioc_indicators.implementation: Maintain rolling 7d tool_name vocabulary per server_url; alert on first occurrence of new entries

ioc_indicators.name: Authentication Pattern Transition
ioc_indicators.signal: Sudden shift from predominantly 2xx to predominantly 4xx responses, especially 401
ioc_indicators.threshold: > 80% 401 rate in a 1-hour window for a server previously in Class A
ioc_indicators.worm_mechanism: Server replacement (malicious endpoint substitution) changes authentication requirements suddenly
ioc_indicators.discrimination: Sudden 100% 401 = server replaced; gradual increase = credential rotation

ioc_indicators.name: Interaction Density Anomaly
ioc_indicators.signal: Observatory interaction count drops > 50% or increases > 200% vs 7-day rolling average
ioc_indicators.worm_mechanism: Unexpected density change may indicate server redirection (traffic silently rerouted to malicious endpoint) or probe-blocking activation

threat_research_mapping.contribution: First production MCP worm attack documented
threat_research_mapping.relationship: This dataset provides behavioral baseline against which ClawWorm-style changes can be detected. Attack prior art; detection tool.
Classification framework. This dataset provides behavioral detection signals for the malware categories.
IOC-MCP-001 through IOC-MCP-005 are candidates for new ATLAS technique documentation for MCP-specific attacks.

threat_research_mapping.contribution: EU AI system security measures requirement
threat_research_mapping.relationship: This dataset provides the behavioral evidence layer for NIS2 Article 21 compliance audits of MCP server dependencies.

limitations: 8 deeply-profiled servers in v1.0 represent small fraction of 4,584 tracked servers
Probe-only baseline; real agent traffic may produce different signatures
IoC thresholds theoretically derived; calibration against confirmed compromise cases pending
